title: GlobalTranslate - Multilanguage server
description: Translate all aspects of your server to the language of the minecraft client
body:
# GlobalTranslate
GlobalTranslate is a Minecraft plugin that allows players to automatically translate chat messages in real time using the Google Cloud Translate API. As a server owner you can translate all aspects of your server using PlacesHolder. This plugin is compatible with Minecraft servers running Bukkit or Spigot. [Video Test](https://imgur.com/ldL0L0m)

### Features
- Automatic translation of messages in the chat
- Commands to translate individual messages
- Players have the ability to choose in which language they want to read the chat or see the server.
- Integration with PlaceholderAPI to translate personalized texts
- Support for colors and gradients in messages
- Easy and customizable configuration
###

### Installation
1. Make sure you have an instance of Minecraft running Bukkit or Spigot.
2. Download the plugin JAR file and place it in the 'plugins' folder of your server.
3. Restart the server for the plugin to load and generate its configuration files.
4. Get a Google Cloud Translate API key and add it to the 'config file.yml` in the 'api-key' line.
5. Customize the settings according to your preferences.
6. Restart the server again for the changes to take effect..
###

## Commands
- '/gc <message>': Translate and send a message globally in the language of each player.
- `/ct reload': Reloads the configuration and the translation files of the plugin.
- '/setlang ph <lang code>': You can modify the language in which you read the server
- '/setlang gc <lang code>': You can change the language you read the messages of the players
###

## Permision
- chattranslator.use: Allows the use of the /gc command.
- chattranslator.reload: Allows the use of the /ct reload command.
- chattranslator.bypasscooldown: Allows players to bypass the /gc command cooldown.
###
